title: Can not ping containers in the same network by name.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Different containers that are in the same network can not resolve by container name. Ping by name does not work; ping by IP does work.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
Starting from a fresh podman (`podman system reset`), on Opensuse Tumbleweed.

2.
Create a network, bring up some containers.
```
$ podman network create net
net
$podman run -itd --network net --name box1 busybox
0ae09947a2a570cf4300a508794b5322083f9198780ddb3eabca8cd63cba9fe0
$podman run -itd --network net --name box2 busybox
09d1b7c031aed08d8457fea6292a911b9ba684d26721870dcf2daa3f3dc8be62
$ podman run -itd --network net --name box3 centos
ab1d90b5b279708b985354c66126682a08c012cd48b2a07c836f0a8fdf20d6ba
```

3. Enter a container and try to ping by container name.
```
$ podman exec -it box3 sh
sh-4.4# ping localhost                                                             # ping itself
PING localhost(localhost (::1)) 56 data bytes
64 bytes from localhost (::1): icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.040 ms
# ...
sh-4.4# ping box3                                                                    # ping itself by contianer name
PING ab1d90b5b279 (10.89.0.4) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from ab1d90b5b279 (10.89.0.4): icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.008 ms
# ...
sh-4.4# ping box2                                                                     # ping other continer by name
ping: box2: Name or service not known
sh-4.4# ping box1                                                                     # ping other continer by name
ping: box1: Name or service not known
sh-4.4# ping 10.89.0.2                                                              # ping other continer by IP
PING 10.89.0.2 (10.89.0.2) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 10.89.0.2: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.037 ms
# ...
sh-4.4# ping www.google.ch                                                      # ping to WAN does also work
PING www.google.ch (142.250.203.99) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from zrh04s16-in-f3.1e100.net (142.250.203.99): icmp_seq=1 ttl=254 time=2.56 ms


# Differnt terminal
$ podman inspect box1 | grep IPAddress                                  # IP of box 1
               "IPAddress": "",
                         "IPAddress": "10.89.0.2",
```



**Describe the results you received:**
Name resolution between containers in the same network does not work. By IP everything is ok.


**Describe the results you expected:**
Name resolution to work.


**Additional information:**
Shouldn't `resolve.conf` be more populated?
```
$ podman exec -it box3 sh
sh-4.4# cat /etc/resolv.conf 
nameserver 192.168.1.1
```
```
$ podman network inspect net
[
     {
          "name": "net",
          "id": "a08a0fcbdeafd3c1b3a4b495b9a9c9d96850f08946b52bc0622347d3b6e73b78",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "cni-podman1",
          "created": "2022-11-10T00:42:09.075707095+01:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": false,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
```


**Output of `podman version`:**
```
$ podman version

Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Wed Sep 21 02:00:00 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, with a repo that provides 4.3

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.1-2.1.x86_64

$ zypper se --installed-only --details podman slirp4netns
S  | Name                     | Type    | Version    | Arch   | Repository
```

comments.body:
podman4 has begun to switch to netavark and aardvark for its network stack.  nevertheless, the information provided is insufficient to debug.

you should know that in order to use name resolution with rootless container, your network must be netavark if I remember correctly.  @Luap99 what say you as well ?

> "dns_enabled": false,

With cni you need to have the dnsname plugin installed and then recreate the network, this will also works rootless.
The problem with dnsname is that it will only work for a single network, when your container is connected to more than one network it will not work as expected. That is why we recommend to use netavark with aardvark-dns instead.
